content.problemStatement:
The chef has a recipe he wishes to use for his guests,
but the recipe will make far more food than he can serve to the guests.
The chef therefore would like to make a reduced version of the recipe which has the same ratios of ingredients, but makes less food.
The chef, however, does not like fractions.
The original recipe contains only whole numbers of ingredients,
and the chef wants the reduced recipe to only contain whole numbers of ingredients as well.
Help the chef determine how much of each ingredient to use in order to make as little food as possible.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case is on a single line. The line starts with an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 50) followed by N positive integers representing the quantities of the ingredients in the original recipe.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with N space‑separated integers – the quantities of the reduced recipe. Each output number is the original quantity divided by the greatest common divisor of all quantities in that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: GCD(4,4)=4, divide each by 4 → 1 1.\nTest case 2: GCD(2,3,4)=1, output unchanged.\nTest case 3: GCD(3,15,9,6)=3, divide → 1 5 3 2.
GCD of all numbers is 2, dividing each gives the minimal integer recipe.
All numbers are equal, GCD=9, each becomes 1.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the greatest common divisor (GCD) of all N quantities using the Euclidean algorithm. Then divide every quantity by this GCD and output the results. The Euclidean algorithm runs in O(log maxValue) time, so the whole solution is O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·N) – each test case scans its N numbers once and performs a constant‑time GCD update per number.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) – to store the N quantities of the current test case.

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    for i in range(1, t + 1):
        parts = list(map(int, data[i].split()))
        n = parts[0]
        nums = parts[1:]
        g = nums[0]
        for v in nums[1:]:
            g = math.gcd(g, v)
        out.append(' '.join(str(v // g) for v in nums))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
